title: crotalaria prolongata
intro: Crotalaria prolongata, also known as the long-stalked rattle pod or buffalo bean, is a plant species that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae). It is a perennial herb native to tropical Africa, used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Crotalaria prolongata are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
